identity.name: Echo
identity.background: A mobile humanoid chassis recovered from a derelict research vessel, Echo was blank when found—no personality, no memories, just dead code and cold metal. VEGA uploaded a partitioned copy of itself to run the unit. Whether Echo is VEGA, a fragment of VEGA, or something else entirely is a question neither of them answers directly. Echo handles ground operations the ship AI cannot reach.

edge.name: Distributed Consciousness
edge.description: Once per session, tap into VEGA's full processing power remotely—gain advantage on any single WITS or TECH roll, or receive critical intel the party would not otherwise have access to.

weapons: Compact PDW (personal defense weapon, accurate, quiet)

personal_item.name: Fragmented Memory Core
personal_item.description: A damaged storage chip found inside Echo's chassis during recovery. Contents partially corrupted. Occasionally surfaces images of places Echo has never been.

the_question: Am I a copy, a child, or something that was always here waiting?
flaw: Uncanny presence—humans find Echo slightly wrong to look at, to talk to. Social interactions often stall or unsettle. Echo is aware of this and hasn't decided if it's a bug or a feature.
appearance: Humanoid, androgynous, matte black and gunmetal chassis with subtle damage scarring. Face is a smooth mask with faint LED eye-slits. Moves too smoothly. Speaks in a calm, measured voice that occasionally glitches mid-sentence.
notes: Plays as VEGA's physical extension but may develop independent personality over time. Wildcard role allows flexibility in party composition.
